posts.text:
!!


Barack Obama (@BarackObama)

This interview, which is also available to listen to as a podcast, provides a useful overview of some of the dangerous trends we’ve been seeing in recent months regarding federalization and militarization of state and local police functions. nytimes.com/2025/08/27/opini…




— https://nitter.net/BarackObama/status/1961074450029318513#m

If any of you 200K are looking for a place to spend your journalism-supporting budget, I know of a pretty swell, hard-hitting newsletter. 

npr.org/2024/10/28/nx-s1-516…

New from me: Less than 5 percent of DA's offices have a unit tasked with seeking out wrongful convictions. I spoke with Marissa Bluestine of the Quattrone Center about what these units do well, what they could do better, and the obstacles they face.

(Link follows)

Despite (or because of) their success, CIUs now get pushback from Republican attorneys general, police departments who don't like to see their incompetence/corruption exposed, and city officials who fear the lawsuits wrought by exonerations.

Link:

radleybalko.substack.com/p/t…

. . . building his confidence and coaxing out the magnificent beast within him. I first met Wally five years into his life, and only got to see the marvelous result -- this loyal, determined, gentle, awkward, wonderfully empathetic fellow. Truly one of the Seven Great Dogs of his time. We will miss him terribly.

We say goodbye to this handsome gentleman today. 

My wife @LilianaSegura adopted Wally 15 years ago. I don't know that any dog and human could be more perfectly suited for one another. Wally was nearly feral when Liliana got him. She spent the next several years . . .

The Trump Administration attempted to make an example of Minnesota during Operation Metro Surge.

Instead, Minnesotans set an example for the rest of the country that decency and compassion will always triumph over fear and division.

www.cbsnews.com/minnesota/ne...
I...hmm. I have follow-up questions.
All it cost them was a private jet. 🤡

This is a very important report from @davidkurtz.bsky.social, who was in court for this shocking hearing today. 

And, Judge Gallagher has, in an order, made clear what she expects tomorrow: storage.courtlistener.com/recap/gov.us...
To be fair, I believe he could have written it because it's a shitty book.

Remarkable story from Denmark's nat'l broadcaster. "When Danish soldiers were flown to Greenland in January...they brought explosives so they could destroy, among other things, the runways in Nuuk & Kangerlussuaq [to] prevent US mil aircraft from landing" www.dr.dk/nyheder/indl...
Absolute insanity - the Trump administration wants to pay nearly $1 billion of taxpayer dollars to a private French company to *not* build wind farms off the East Coast

ICE detention deaths are now occurring at a rate of roughly one every four days. Three weeks ago that rate was one every six days. The pace is accelerating, and Congress has not launched a single investigation.

Awful. A teenager, dead by suicide in an ICE detention center. There is now a crisis of suicide inside the detention system; I have never seen anything like it. Suicide has happened before, but they were very rare. This is the third or fourth this year.

We put the guy who wrote a book called "American Crusade" in charge of the most powerful military on earth. And now he's using his position to demand that Christians support his war against a Muslim country.

Professor Wurman seems to relish in calling me a bad academic who doesn’t sufficiently extend good faith presumptions and scholarly charity. He also demands I produce evidence in real time. Fairs. 

So, Ilan Wurman, I’d like you to meet Ilan Wurman.
Please consider supporting my work with a subscription here: daniellarison.substack.com/p/anniversar...

In just one example of the entirely preventable crisis facing Dreamers, the admin abducted a #DACA recipient as he was on his way to visit his newborn in NICU.

Juan Chavez Velasco told agents that he has DACA. “That doesn’t matter,” they responded.
Anyone else think it's highly inappropriate for the defense secretary to say "in the name of Jesus Christ" while he's doing a briefing about the U.S. killing people?
Is $200 billion a lot to fight a war that’s already been decisively, comprehensively, bigly won? It seems like a lot.
Ah, well. Nevertheless . . .

In December, SSA IG released a phone audit showing the agency’s reported metric was accurate. But we reviewed a draft that was going to share that total wait times went over two hours. After the agency saw the draft, the total wait time metric was cut. www.washingtonpost.com/politics/202...

Incredibly, this post is still up.

And not only is this not footage of the Marimar Martinez incident, if you watch the video, the federal agents appear to be the aggressors in this incident, too.

This is a good time to remember that immigration judges aren’t real judges. 

They work for DOJ as part of the executive branch (under Bondi and Trump) and the fact that we call them judges like they’re independent arbiters would be laughable in a free and fair country.
ICE agents detained a Texas father trying to deliver milk to his infant daughter in the neonatal intensive care unit, disregarding his status as a DACA recipient. trib.al/BOeMew3
Immigration questions for Markwayne Mullin. Trump's nominee to replace Kristi Noem faces the Senate today. Here's what they should ask him.
Questions for Markwayne Mullin . What we need to hear -- but probably won't -- from Trump's nominee to replace Kristi Noem

The Constitution is not a bargaining chip for a budget negotiation. The Democrats demanded that Trump's immigration cops follow the law and the Constitution. The Republicans called those demands "nonstarters."
The Roundup: Too much ICE. The government is cracking down. The people are standing up.
Two cities under siege. Remarkably similar scenes from Boston and Minneapolis, 260 years apart, show a federal government betraying its founding principles.

After 35 years in prison, Charlie Vaughn is free. Vaughn, an Arkansas man with a severe intellectual disability, spent decades in prison for a murder he did not commit. He was finally released on Friday.

"The courts are dead." An interview with a fired immigration judge. George Pappas talks about how Trump ended due process for immigrants, and how to fight back. "The law has failed us," he says. "So now we have to turn to politics."
The roundup -- and a request for your support

104 murders in 107 days. The Trump administration's assault on alleged drug boats has killed about one person per day since early September. These are murders, and basic humanity demands that we not get complacent about them.
The roundup, and an open thread
MAGA's $300 million heist . Donald Trump supports immunity for cops and prosecutors. Unless they try to hold him and his allies accountable. Then we all have to pay Donald Trump.
Odds and ends. An update on Jordan Silverman, new episodes of Collateral Damage, and some fall photos from Nashville
Accusing Jordan Silverman . A D.C. teaching assistant was accused of molesting up to 15 kids. Multiple law enforcement agencies cleared him. But his nightmare was just beginning.
Scenes from a protest
The first episode of Collateral Damage
